CRIME.actionStep: Stay alert and avoid the affected area if possible.
steps: Report any suspicious activity to local police
Avoid travelling alone in the affected area
Share your live location with a trusted contact
Keep emergency contacts readily accessible

WEATHER.actionStep: Monitor official weather updates and prepare for severe conditions.
steps: Stock up on essential supplies (water, food, batteries)
Secure loose outdoor objects and close windows
Identify the nearest shelter or safe room
Keep a charged phone and portable charger ready

HEALTH.actionStep: Follow official health advisories and take preventive measures.
steps: Wash hands frequently and maintain hygiene
Avoid crowded places if an outbreak is reported
Consult a healthcare professional if symptoms appear
Keep a basic first-aid kit and essential medications at home

SCAM.actionStep: Do not respond to suspicious messages or share personal information.
steps: Verify the sender through official channels before acting
Never share OTPs, passwords, or banking details
Report the scam to local cyber crime authorities
Warn family and friends about the scam pattern

CYBER.actionStep: Disconnect affected systems and change compromised credentials immediately.
steps: Update all passwords for potentially affected accounts
Run a full antivirus and malware scan
Enable two-factor authentication where possible
Report the incident to your IT department or CERT

OTHER.actionStep: Stay informed and follow guidance from local authorities.
steps: Monitor official news sources for updates
Follow instructions from emergency services
Share verified information with your community
